audit.icydb_build_profile: workspace test/dev validation with --all-features; existing performance artifacts are PocketIC instruction reports

top_immediate_wins: Push cursor boundaries into deterministic index-backed ordered pages.
Add streaming visitors for recursive value-storage projection and validation.
Surface exact index cardinality and materialization facts in explain/diagnostics.
Add an optional SQLite STRICT comparison benchmark harness.
top_strategic_wins: Use explicit IC-native query-plan facts with deterministic exact statistics.
Prototype offset-addressable row storage before persisted adoption.
Productize durable schema migration state before broad DDL.
Write a byte-level stable-memory format specification.
Add SQLite differential and durability fault-injection harnesses.
summary.biggest_correctness_risk: Schema mutation and recovery edge cases once migration support expands beyond the current fail-closed supported path.
summary.biggest_performance_risk: Bounded ordered reads that materialize or read many candidates before applying page boundaries.
summary.biggest_testing_gap: Differential plus fault-injection testing across query, index, persisted decode, and recovery paths.

findings.title: Cursor boundaries are applied after access/materialization
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite B-tree cursor/range access and ORDER BY/LIMIT avoidance principles.
findings.recommended_fix: Implement cursor boundary pushdown for deterministic index-backed ordered reads, starting with order-compatible single-index plans and stop-after-limit execution.
acceptance_criteria: Forward and reverse page tests show no duplicates or skipped rows.
Row reads for matching LIMIT 1/3/10 pages scale with page size.
Explain output reports whether cursor handling was pushed down or post-access.
findings.risk: Pagination correctness regressions around equal keys, tombstones, reverse scans, or live/journaled overlays.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Implement cursor boundary pushdown for deterministic index-backed ordered reads. Limit the first slice to order-compatible single-index plans, add page-stability tests, and extend explain/perf attribution to show pushed versus post-access cursor handling.

findings.title: Recursive value-storage decode allocates projected containers
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite serial-record discipline permits field-aware access without constructing unnecessary application values.
findings.recommended_fix: Add streaming or borrowed visitors for recursive list/map decode and migrate projection-only validation paths away from owned Value allocation.
acceptance_criteria: Owned decode and streaming visitor produce identical values/errors.
Projection tests prove unselected recursive fields are not materialized.
Microbench records allocation reduction for nested projection/validation.
findings.risk: Decode error path or malformed-data diagnostics could regress.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Add streaming visitors for recursive structural value-storage list/map decode, wire projection/validation paths to avoid owned Value allocation where possible, and add allocation-sensitive tests or benches.

findings.title: There is no runnable fair SQLite comparison harness
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite performance practice relies on reproducible benchmarks and explicit durability/configuration settings.
icydb_evidence: No project benches found after pruning .cache, target, and .git
findings.recommended_fix: Create an optional benchmark harness that compares overlapping IcyDB and SQLite STRICT scenarios with explicit WAL/synchronous modes and fairness notes.
acceptance_criteria: Harness skips cleanly when SQLite is unavailable.
Insert/read/range/filter/order/count/update/delete scenarios are covered.
Output includes median, p95 or min/max, ratio, and fairness notes.
findings.risk: Misleading benchmark framing if unsafe SQLite modes are treated as headline results.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Create an optional SQLite/IcyDB comparison benchmark harness using STRICT SQLite schemas and IcyDB typed equivalents. Record environment metadata, WAL modes, median/p95 timings, and clear fairness notes; skip gracefully when SQLite is unavailable.

findings.title: Exact cardinality metadata is not yet a first-class planner/diagnostic signal
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite uses statistics to guide planning; IcyDB can use synchronized exact index cardinality deterministically.
findings.recommended_fix: Surface exact per-index prefix counts in explain and diagnostics, then use synchronized counts for deterministic admission/ranking where safe.
acceptance_criteria: Explain shows exact, stale, or unavailable cardinality status.
Diagnostics expose bounded per-index cardinality summaries.
Planner/admission tests use only synchronized exact counts.
findings.risk: Stale cardinality could mislead plans unless generation checks fail closed.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Promote exact index cardinality metadata into query explain and bounded diagnostics, then add deterministic planner/admission tests that only use synchronized exact counts.

findings.title: Ordered small-limit paths still materialize excessive work
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite avoids temporary sorting and stops early when an index satisfies ORDER BY plus LIMIT.
findings.recommended_fix: Add explain diagnostics for materialized ORDER BY + LIMIT and then expand order-compatible composite/covering plan support where measurements justify it.
acceptance_criteria: Explain output identifies materialized order/window paths.
Suggested index shape is shown where deterministic.
At least one top perf-matrix ordered limit row improves after a targeted path.
findings.estimated_effort: S for diagnostics, M/L for broader plan support
findings.risk: Choosing a filter index over an order index can improve one workload and hurt another without exact counts.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Add explain diagnostics for ORDER BY + LIMIT paths that materialize ordering, including exact candidate count when available and a suggested composite index shape; keep public admission behavior unchanged.

findings.title: Fast-path ownership still has documented unguarded areas
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite-style optimizer work needs regression tests that lock down fast-path ownership.
findings.recommended_fix: Add ownership/tripwire tests for stream precedence helpers, grouped fast paths, and bytes-terminal derivation.
acceptance_criteria: Fast-path inventory links each formerly unguarded area to tests.
Tests fail when the wrong path owns or bypasses the fast path.
No production behavior change is required.
findings.risk: Tests can become brittle if they assert exact instruction counts instead of ownership labels.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Close the remaining unguarded entries in docs/governance/fast-path-inventory.md by adding ownership/tripwire tests for stream precedence, grouped fast paths, and bytes-terminal derivation.

findings.title: Durable schema migration execution is not yet ready for broad DDL
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite schema metadata/versioning discipline maps to a durable IC-native migration state machine.
findings.recommended_fix: Before enabling broader mutation kinds, add durable migration id, phase, watermark, publication, validation, and recovery tests for one supported mutation kind.
acceptance_criteria: Migration state survives interruption at every phase.
Old schema remains readable until publish or failure is fail-closed.
Index rebuild validates before publication.
Migration semantics are documented.
findings.risk: A too-general framework could delay useful narrow migration support.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Design and implement the first durable schema migration state slice for one supported mutation kind, with migration id, phase, watermark, publish point, recovery tests, and documentation.

findings.title: Durability health lacks marker/journal/fold diagnostics
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite exposes operational journal/WAL state; IcyDB needs bounded IC-native durability health.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a bounded DurabilityHealth diagnostic containing marker state, journal tail sequence/chunk state, fold watermark, and recovery readiness/last phase.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostic does not scan unbounded row data.
Clean, marker-present, journal-tail, and recovered states are tested.
API lives under diagnostics and is documented as operational introspection.
findings.risk: Exposing internal fields can become API debt; keep it diagnostic and bounded.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Add a bounded durability health diagnostic report that surfaces commit marker state, journal tail/fold-watermark state, recovery readiness, and tests for clean, dirty, and recovered stores.

findings.title: Persisted format policy is explicit but byte-level format docs are missing
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite publishes a detailed file-format specification; IcyDB should document its stable-memory surfaces in its own terms.
findings.recommended_fix: Create a byte-level stable storage format spec mapping schema, data, indexes, journal chunks, commit marker, versioning, and fail-closed decode rules to code/tests.
acceptance_criteria: Each durable surface links to implementation and tests.
Spec states versioning and pre-1.0 hard-cut policy.
Future persisted-format changes are required to update the spec.
findings.risk: Spec could fossilize unstable pre-1.0 details unless clearly labeled.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Draft a byte-level persisted storage format spec that maps every durable stable-memory surface to code, tests, version/fail-closed rules, and pre-1.0 compatibility policy.

findings.title: Query-side metrics are intentionally not surfaced, leaving an observability gap
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN maps to query-safe explain/analyze output for IcyDB.
findings.recommended_fix: Return operation-local explain/analyze metrics without mutating state, and optionally add update-lane snapshots for trusted diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria: Query calls do not mutate canister state.
Analyze output includes plan, index, rows read/returned, cache hit/miss, and materialization flags.
Tests prove metrics collection does not change results or state.
findings.risk: Analyze itself can become expensive; keep it explicit and bounded.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Add a query-safe explain/analyze result that returns operation-local read metrics without mutating canister state, with tests proving state invariance.

findings.title: Caches are bounded but fixed FIFO and weakly tunable
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite page/prepared-statement cache principles map to visible, measured IcyDB plan-cache behavior.
findings.recommended_fix: Expose cache hit/miss/eviction diagnostics and capacity configuration or documentation before considering LRU.
acceptance_criteria: Cache hit/miss/eviction reasons are visible in diagnostics or explain.
Capacity is configurable or clearly documented.
Repeated same-shape benchmark scenarios exist.
findings.risk: Configuration surface can grow without evidence; diagnostics should come first.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Expose bounded query/SQL cache capacity and hit/miss/eviction diagnostics, then add repeated-shape benchmark scenarios before considering an LRU policy.

findings.title: Differential testing against SQLite is only a design idea today
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite testing discipline supports independent-oracle and randomized testing.
icydb_evidence: No committed differential SQLite harness found by repository search
findings.recommended_fix: Build a small random operation harness over SQLite STRICT schemas and IcyDB typed equivalents, classifying mismatches explicitly.
acceptance_criteria: Random insert/update/delete/get/range/filter/count/sort/limit sequences run on both engines.
Harness uses only overlapping semantics and explicit ordering.
Failure output includes seed and operation sequence.
findings.risk: False positives if semantic mapping is too broad.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Implement a small SQLite STRICT differential test harness for users/posts/events with random insert/update/delete/query sequences and explicit mismatch classification.

findings.title: Fault-injection, corruption, and OOM-style tests remain below SQLite's bar
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite systematically tests crashes, I/O errors, OOM, and malformed databases.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a test-only phase-by-phase recovery fault harness for marker, journal, fold, live rebuild, index rebuild, marker clear, and decode failures.
acceptance_criteria: Each injected phase recovers to old/new state or fails closed with typed diagnostics.
Integrity report passes after successful recovery.
Corrupted persisted bytes fail closed without mutating state.
findings.risk: Test hooks can pollute production code; keep them cfg(test) and minimal.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Add a test-only recovery fault-injection harness that interrupts each commit/recovery phase and asserts fail-closed or fully recovered invariants.

findings.title: Public read admission is strong, but performance guidance needs index-design examples
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite planner docs teach users how indexes affect query behavior.
findings.recommended_fix: Write an IcyDB query performance guide covering equality-prefix indexes, range bounds, order compatibility, covering projections, cursor pagination, and admission failures.
acceptance_criteria: Guide includes good/bad index-shape examples.
Examples link to explain output and read-admission behavior.
Examples compile or are tied to tested fixtures where feasible.
findings.risk: Documentation can drift unless examples are connected to tests.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Write a query performance and index-design guide for IcyDB's deterministic planner, including equality-prefix, range, ORDER BY + LIMIT, covering, cursor, and read-admission examples.

findings.title: cargo bench and allocation regression gates are absent
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite uses performance regression discipline alongside correctness testing.
icydb_evidence: No project benches found after pruning third-party/cache directories
Ignored PocketIC performance report tests are present
findings.recommended_fix: Add a minimal native cargo benchmark target with allocation counters for PK get, indexed range, covering projection, recursive decode, and plan-cache reuse.
acceptance_criteria: cargo bench runs the selected hot paths.
Decode/projection scenarios record allocation counts.
Docs label native benches as complements to PocketIC instruction reports.
findings.risk: Native microbenchmarks may not predict wasm/stable-memory performance.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Add a small native cargo benchmark target for IcyDB hot paths with allocation counters, explicitly labeled as a complement to PocketIC instruction benchmarks.

findings.title: Checksums/import/export need a 1.0 decision record
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite's documented file/recovery semantics highlight the need for explicit IcyDB backup/corruption decisions.
findings.recommended_fix: Write a 1.0 decision record for checksums, raw export/import, logical export/import, repair scope, and CLI health commands.
acceptance_criteria: Decision classifies each item as copy, adapt IC-natively, or reject.
If checksums are deferred, alternative detection is documented.
CLI/tooling backlog is updated.
findings.risk: Premature checksum format can create migration burden.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Write a 1.0 durability tooling decision record for checksums, raw/logical export-import, repair scope, and CLI health commands.

findings.title: Offset-addressable row storage is the highest-leverage strategic performance bet
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite's serial record format shows the value of compact field-aware row access; IcyDB needs an IC-native variant.
findings.recommended_fix: Prototype an offset-addressable row codec in benchmarks/tests before persisted-format adoption.
acceptance_criteria: Old/new row codecs are benchmarked for full decode, projected fields, nested fields, and malformed input.
Persisted-format policy is updated before any durable adoption.
No pre-1.0 compatibility fallback is retained unless explicitly required.
findings.risk: Persisted format churn and migration complexity.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Prototype an offset-addressable row codec in tests/benchmarks only, compare decode/projection allocation and instruction cost, then prepare a persisted-format policy update if it wins.

findings.title: IcyDB should not copy several SQLite mechanisms unchanged
findings.sqlite_reference: SQLite's POSIX file, locking, temp file, dynamic typing, and broad SQL design serve a different environment.
findings.recommended_fix: Document which SQLite ideas IcyDB copies, adapts IC-natively, or rejects.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture docs explicitly reject POSIX fsync assumptions, temp disk spill, dynamic typing, broad SQL feature parity, and unsafe speed PRAGMAs as default guidance.
Roadmap items tie to IC-specific success metrics.
findings.risk: Over-documentation if not kept short and linked to existing contracts.
findings.follow_up_patch_prompt: Add a short architecture note classifying SQLite ideas IcyDB should copy, adapt IC-natively, or reject, with links to resource model and SQL subset docs.

backlog.title: Cursor-boundary pushdown for index-backed ordered pages
backlog.expected_impact: Large instruction reduction for small paginated reads.
acceptance_criteria: Row reads scale with limit.
No duplicates or skipped rows across pages.
Explain reports pushed versus post-access cursor.

backlog.title: Streaming recursive value decode visitors
backlog.expected_impact: Lower allocation and instruction cost for projection and validation.
acceptance_criteria: Decode parity tests pass.
Projection avoids unselected recursive allocation.
Allocation benchmark improves.

backlog.title: SQLite STRICT differential harness skeleton
backlog.expected_impact: Independent oracle for overlapping query/index semantics.
acceptance_criteria: Random operation sequences run on both engines.
Mismatch classes are explicit.
Failures include seed and operation log.

backlog.title: Fast-path tripwire tests
backlog.expected_impact: Protects recent optimization work from regression.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory unguarded entries have test links.
Tests assert ownership labels or path facts.

backlog.title: Query explain cardinality/materialization facts
backlog.expected_impact: Makes expensive plan causes visible and supports deterministic planner improvements.
acceptance_criteria: Explain includes exact/stale/unavailable cardinality.
Explain includes order/cursor materialization flags.

backlog.title: Durability health diagnostic
backlog.expected_impact: Improves operator visibility into marker, journal, fold, and recovery state.
acceptance_criteria: Clean, dirty, and recovered states are tested.
Report is bounded.

backlog.title: Optional SQLite/IcyDB benchmark harness
backlog.expected_impact: Provides fair comparative bottleneck evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Skips cleanly when SQLite is unavailable.
Records WAL/synchronous modes and environment metadata.

backlog.title: Query performance and index-design guide
backlog.expected_impact: Helps users write bounded performant queries.
acceptance_criteria: Examples cover equality-prefix, range, order, limit, covering, and admission failures.

backlog.title: Stable storage byte-format spec scaffold
backlog.expected_impact: Improves persisted-format review and upgrade safety.
acceptance_criteria: Every durable surface maps to code and tests.
Pre-1.0 hard-cut policy is explicit.

backlog.title: Recovery fault-injection harness
backlog.expected_impact: Raises confidence in marker/journal/recovery invariants.
acceptance_criteria: Every injected phase recovers or fails closed.
Integrity report passes after recovery.

backlog.title: Materialized ORDER BY + LIMIT explain hints
backlog.expected_impact: Gives immediate feedback for top perf hotspots.
acceptance_criteria: Explain includes materialized order flag and suggested index shape when deterministic.

backlog.title: Cache hit/miss/eviction diagnostics
backlog.expected_impact: Improves repeated-query tuning.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostics expose hit/miss/eviction reasons.
Repeated-shape benchmark exists.

backlog.title: Native hot-path cargo bench target
backlog.expected_impact: Catches allocation regressions before full PocketIC runs.
acceptance_criteria: cargo bench covers PK, range, decode, projection, and cache paths.
Native scope is documented.

backlog.title: Persisted decode fuzz target
backlog.expected_impact: Finds malformed persisted-format decode bugs.
acceptance_criteria: Malformed bytes fail closed.
No state mutation occurs after decode failure.

backlog.title: Durable migration state first slice
backlog.expected_impact: Enables safe schema mutation growth.
acceptance_criteria: Migration id, phase, watermark, publish point, and recovery tests exist.

backlog.title: Offset row codec prototype
backlog.expected_impact: Potentially large projection/filter decode win.
acceptance_criteria: Old/new codecs are benchmarked.
Persisted adoption waits for policy update.

backlog.title: Covering-index projection expansion
backlog.expected_impact: Reduces row reads for projection queries.
acceptance_criteria: Index path equals scan path.
Row-presence checks stay explicit.

backlog.title: Read-admission exact-count option
backlog.expected_impact: Rejects known-expensive reads earlier.
acceptance_criteria: Only synchronized exact counts drive admission.
Stale metadata fails closed.

backlog.title: CLI durability check/export/import decision
backlog.expected_impact: Clarifies 1.0 operator story.
acceptance_criteria: Decision covers checksums, raw/logical export-import, repair, and CLI health commands.

backlog.title: Macro compile-error polish audit
backlog.expected_impact: Improves developer experience.
acceptance_criteria: Trybuild tests cover common schema/macro mistakes with actionable messages.

backlog.title: Query-safe analyze API
backlog.expected_impact: Provides per-call plan metrics without mutating query state.
acceptance_criteria: State-invariance tests pass.
Analyze output is bounded and explicit.

backlog.title: Stable memory read/write counter scenarios
backlog.expected_impact: Makes IC-specific bottlenecks visible.
acceptance_criteria: Key scenarios report stable reads/writes and bytes.

backlog.title: Storage fragmentation/free-space report
backlog.expected_impact: Detects durable bloat and compaction needs.
acceptance_criteria: Bounded estimate is documented and tested.

backlog.title: Feature and configuration guide
backlog.expected_impact: Clarifies performance/correctness trade-offs.
acceptance_criteria: Feature flags and defaults are documented in one guide.

backlog.title: SQLite copy/adapt/reject architecture note
backlog.expected_impact: Prevents inappropriate SQLite feature copying.
acceptance_criteria: Architecture note classifies SQLite principles as copy, adapt, or reject.
